_comment2: Edit the values below. Do not remove any keys.

matching._comment2: fuzzy_enabled: use fuzzy matching for artist/album/title (catches slight tag differences)
matching._comment4: use_duration: include track duration in matching to reduce false positives
matching._comment5: duration_tolerance_seconds: how many seconds apart two tracks can be and still match
matching._comment6: exact_match_size_tolerance_percent: max file size difference (%) for auto-move as exact match.
matching._comment6b: Files at the same bitrate differ in size only due to embedded tags/artwork - not audio.
matching._comment6c: 3.0% catches nearly all such cases safely. 0 = byte-for-byte exact only.

performance._comment: max_threads: number of parallel threads for scanning. 4-8 recommended. 0 = auto (CPU count)
performance._comment2: cache_enabled: cache organized folder scan to speed up future runs
performance._comment3: cache_file: where to store the metadata cache.

resume._comment: If the script is interrupted, it can resume from where it left off.

output._comment: Where logs and CSV reports are saved. Leave blank to use the duplicates folder.

acoustid._comment2: Catches duplicates with different filenames or tags (renamed / retagged files).
acoustid._comment3: No API key required - fingerprints are compared locally.
acoustid._comment4: Download fpcalc from https://acoustid.org/chromaprint and add it to your PATH.
acoustid._comment5: enabled: set to true to be offered fingerprint matching at each run startup.
acoustid._comment6: fpcalc_path: path to the fpcalc executable. 'fpcalc' works if it is in your PATH.
acoustid._comment7: similarity_threshold: 0-100. How similar fingerprints must be to count as a match.
acoustid._comment7b: 85 is recommended. Lower = more matches but more false positives.
acoustid._comment8: fp_cache_file: where to cache fingerprints. Avoids re-reading audio on future runs.
